<h2>Supplemental Information: Episode 8</h2>

  <div class="supplement" id="e5.8">
    <h3 class="supplement-title">One Block to Rule Them All: The Single-Block Form in African Music</h3>
    <h4>Alice (Bai) Xue (Peabody Institute of The Johns Hopkins University)</h4>
    <h5>Release Date: Thursday, June 11, 2026</h5>
    <div class="floatsupps">
      <div style="float:left; width: 40%;">
        <img class="biopic" alt="Xue Headshot" src="../supplements/S05Ep08xue.jpg">

        <p style="clear:both; padding-top: 10px;"><a href="../supplements/S05Ep08Supp_xue.pdf" target="_blank">Supplemental Materials (PDF)</a>
        </p>

        <p style="font-size: 12px;">

      </div>
      <div style="float:right; width: 60%;">
        <p><strong>Bio:</strong> Alice (Bai) Xue earned her PhD in Music Theory from the CUNY Graduate Center and currently teaches at New York University. Her research spans a wide range of interests, with a current focus on form and structure in African traditional music. Outside of her academic work, Alice is an active musician, a trumpet player who performs in the vibrant music scenes of New York City.</p>

        <p><strong>Keywords:</strong> <em>African music; musical form; repetition; single-block form; call-and-response</em></p>
      </div>
